title: Software QA Working Student
description: Execute structured test runs and support release-readiness activities to ensure stable, reliable, and high-quality software.
Collaborate closely with developers to reproduce defects, assess risks, validate fixes, and strengthen overall product quality.
Contribute to R&D initiatives by evaluating new features, identifying improvement opportunities, and providing quality-focused feedback throughout development.
Create and maintain test cases, test runs, QA documentation, and internal wiki content for consistent and traceable testing.
Contribute to internal company projects while continuously improving QA workflows, test coverage, and team processes.
Train and support new working students in testing tools, quality standards, and established QA practices.

title: Independent Projects & Continuous Learning
description: Turned curiosity into personal projects, prototypes, scripts, and experiments—usually starting with, “What happens if I try this?”
Explored software, AI, automation, and product ideas by building them, breaking them, and rebuilding them better.
Developed a natural QA mindset long before the job title: questioning assumptions, finding edge cases, and spotting opportunities for improvement.
Collected practical skills, failed experiments, and countless debugging lessons—the unofficial experience that prepared me for the official role.
